etymology.description: 夊("foot") + 𡕒("foot") = two feet facing the same direction
etymology.interpretation: Originally depicted two feet facing the same direction, indicative of alignment or a specific movement. This contrasts with similar characters where feet face different directions, suggesting divergence or a change in direction. The character captures the concept of feet in synchronized motion, highlighting its thematic link to physical orientation.

parts.english: foot; part of an ancient form

examples.chinese: 他的计划总是出点儿小舛(他的計劃總是出點兒小舛)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de jìhuà zǒng shì chū diǎnr xiǎo chuǎn.
examples.english: His plans always encounter small setbacks.

examples.chinese: 尽管有舛错(儘管有舛錯)，他仍然达到了目标(他仍然達到了目標)。
examples.pinyin: Jǐnguǎn yǒu chuǎncuò, tā réngrán dádào le mùbiāo.
examples.english: Despite the mistakes, he still achieved his goal.

examples.chinese: 为了避免舛误(為了避免舛誤)，我们仔细检查了所有的数据(我們仔細檢查了所有的數據)。
examples.pinyin: Wèile bìmiǎn chuǎnwù, wǒmen zǐxì jiǎnchá le suǒyǒu de shùjù.
examples.english: To avoid errors, we carefully checked all the data.
